response:
Sec. 1  
[THE WRITER IS CONSIDERING TWO PUBLISHER OFFERS BASED ON SALES REVENUE.]  

Sec. 2  
[PUBLISHER A OFFERS A FLAT FEE OF 3000 DOLLARS PLUS 5 PERCENT OF THE BOOKS SALES REVENUE.]  

Sec. 3  
[PUBLISHER B OFFERS A FLAT FEE OF 1500 DOLLARS PLUS 10 PERCENT OF THE BOOKS SALES REVENUE.]  

Sec. 4  
[TO DETERMINE WHEN PUBLISHER A IS MORE LUCRATIVE THAN PUBLISHER B AN INEQUALITY MUST BE FORMED.]  

Sec. 5  
[THE INEQUALITY IS 3000 PLUS 0.05X GREATER THAN 1500 PLUS 0.10X.]  

Sec. 6  
[SOLVING THIS INEQUALITY LEADS TO THE RANGE OF X WHERE PUBLISHER AS OFFER IS BETTER.]  

Sec. 7  
[WHEN THE ANTICIPATED SALES REVENUE IS BETWEEN 10000 AND 40000 DOLLARS PUBLISHER AS OFFER IS MORE BENEFICIAL FOR X LESS THAN 30000.]  

Sec. 8  
[THEREFORE THE WRITER SHOULD CHOOSE PUBLISHER A IF SHE EXPECTS SALES REVENUE TO BE LESS THAN 30000 DOLLARS.]
